messages.content: 用英文拟一封邮件给老师Leyla De Amicis说明刚刚上课签到失败，以为下课会签到，希望补签到的内容

Subject: Issue with Attendance in Today's Class

Dear Professor <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to inform you that I encountered some difficulties with the attendance process during today's class. I mistakenly assumed that the attendance would be taken at the end of the session and did not sign in at the beginning of the class.

I apologize for any inconvenience and I would like to request your assistance in rectifying this situation. Please let me know the necessary steps or any additional information required for me to make up for the missed attendance. I am keen on ensuring that my presence in class is accurately recorded for academic purposes.

Thank you for your understanding and consideration. I appreciate your patience in resolving this matter.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
中国未来一百年不会倒闭的二十家上市公司

1. Alibaba Group Holding Ltd (BABA)
2. Tencent Holdings Ltd (0700.HK)
3. Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd.
4. Ping An Insurance Group Co. of China Ltd (2318.HK)
5. Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd (601398.SS)
6. China Construction Bank Corp (601939.SS)
7. JD.com, Inc. (JD)
8. Baidu, Inc. (BIDU)
9. China Mobile Ltd (0941.HK)
10. PetroChina Company Limited (601857.SS)
11. China Petroleum & Chemical Corporation (Sinopec) (0386.HK)
12. China Life Insurance Company Limited (2628.HK)
13. CNOOC Limited (0883.HK)
14. Midea Group Co., Ltd. (000333.SZ)
15. BYD Co., Ltd. (1211.HK)
16. Hikvision Digital Technology Co., Ltd. (002415.SZ)
17. Gree Electric Appliances, Inc. of Zhuhai (000651.SZ)
18. China Evergrande Group (3333.HK)
19. China Vanke Co., Ltd. (000002.SZ)
20. Air China Limited (0753.HK)

Please note that this is just a list based on current market conditions and trends, and future events and changes in the business landscape may affect the performance and sustainability of these companies.
依据是什么，中国恒大已经暴雷了

非常抱歉，恒大暴雷的消息我没有及时更新。恒大集团的确面临着一些困境，包括高额的债务和流动性问题。这个情况可能会对该公司的未来产生一定的影响。

关于给出的名单，我选择了一些在当前的商业环境下运作良好且有稳定表现的中国上市公司。然而，请注意这只是基于现有市场条件和趋势的推测，并不能完全确保这些公司在未来一百年内都不会倒闭。企业面临各种内外部因素，包括市场变化、经济波动和竞争等，这些因素可能会对其业务和发展产生重要影响。

也就是说，任何关于未来长期时间范围内公司的预测都存在一定的不确定性和风险。在投资或评估任何公司时，综合考虑多种因素并保持谨慎和理性是非常重要的。感谢您的理解。
中国农业银行为什么不在名单之列

非常抱歉没有将中国农业银行（601288.SS）列入上述名单中。中国农业银行是中国四大国有商业银行之一，拥有广泛的业务范围和客户基础。作为一家重要的金融机构，它在中国经济中扮演着重要的角色。

尽管中国农业银行在中国银行业具有重要地位，但在关于未来能否持续经营的预测方面，存在着一定的不确定性和风险。无论是在国内还是国际经济环境中，金融行业都受到多种因素的影响，包括政策变化、市场竞争、经济波动和技术进步等。这些因素都可能对银行的业务和运营产生重要的影响。

提醒再次强调，给出的名单只是一个基于当前市场条件和趋势的推测，并不能确保公司在未来一百年内都不会发生变故。在进行任何投资或评估时，请综合考虑多种因素并保持谨慎和理性的态度。

感谢您的指出，希望我的回答能对您有所帮助。
中国在基础民生领域各个行业的龙头公司

以下是中国基础民生领域各个行业的一些领头企业，仅供参考：

1. 零售商业：万科集团 (000002.SZ)、恒隆地产 (0101.HK)、北京华联超市 (000882.SZ)、永辉超市 (601933.SS)、大润发超市 (002385.SZ)
2. 银行业：中国工商银行 (601398.SS)、中国银行 (601988.SS)、中国建设银行 (601939.SS)、中国农业银行 (601288.SS)、招商银行 (600036.SS)
3. 能源供应：中国石油化工集团 (Sinopec) (0386.HK)、中国化工集团 (601117.SS)、中海油 (CNOOC) (0883.HK)、中国石油天然气股份有限公司 (PetroChina) (601857.SS)
4. 电信和通讯：中国移动 (0941.HK)、中国电信 (0728.HK)、中国联通 (0762.HK)、华为技术有限公司、中兴通讯股份有限公司 (000063.SZ)
5. 基础设施建设：中国交通建设集团 (601800.SS)、中国铁建 (601186.SS)、中国水电建设集团 (601669.SS)、中国电力建设集团 (601669.SS)
6. 房地产开发：万科集团 (000002.SZ)、恒大地产 (3333.HK)、碧桂园控股有限公司 (02007.HK)、绿地控股集团有限公司 (03900.HK)、中国保利集团 (600048.SS)
7. 快递物流：顺丰控股有限公司 (002352.SZ)、中通快递 (6030.HK)、申通快递股份有限公司 (002468.SZ)、圆通速递 (600233.SS)、韵达速递股份有限公司 (002120.SZ)

这些公司在各自的领域内具有较强的市场地位和领导优势。请注意，这只是一个简单的示例，不代表全部行业中的所有龙头公司。行业领导地位可能会因时间和市场环境的变化而发生变化，请在进行投资或评估时衡量各种因素并保持谨慎。
The purpose of visualisation for exploratory data analysis is:

The purpose of visualization for exploratory data analysis is to gain insights, discover patterns, and identify relationships or trends in the data. By visually representing the data using various charts, graphs, or plots, analysts can effectively explore and understand the underlying structure and characteristics of the data set. Visualization can help in identifying outliers, examining the distribution of variables, highlighting correlations, and uncovering hidden patterns that may not be apparent in raw data. It allows for the detection of anomalies, data quality issues, and potential hypotheses to be tested. Ultimately, visualization aids in generating hypotheses, formulating questions, and guiding further data exploration and analysis.

The purpose of visualisation for exploratory data analysis is:

Question 1Select one:

a.
To learn about what you can generalise to the wider population


b.
To simply test a hypothesis


c.
To provide your final formatted plots for reporting


d.
To recognise potential patterns or problems in the data
The purpose of visualization for exploratory data analysis is to recognize potential patterns or problems in the data.

The file drawer problem is a result of…
Question 2Select one:

a.
When researchers fail to report all of a study’s dependent measures

b.
When researchers decide whether to exclude data after looking at the impact of doing so on the results

c.
When researchers selectively report studies that “worked"

d.
When researchers fail to report all of a study’s conditions

The correct answer is c. When researchers selectively report studies that “worked". The file drawer problem refers to the bias in the publication and reporting of research studies, where studies with statistically significant or positive results are more likely to be published, while studies with non-significant or negative results are less likely to be published or remain in the researcher's file drawer. This selective reporting can lead to an inaccurate representation of the overall body of research on a particular topic.

Which of the following is a non-directional hypothesis?
Question 3Select one:

a.
We hypothesise that participants who drink more than 10 units of alcohol per week will report lower quality sleep on the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index

b.
We hypothesise that Scottish participants will produce more swear words than English participants on a vocabulary test

c.
We hypothesise that students who do not attend their psychology lectures will obtain a lower grade on their final psychology exam

d.
We hypothesise that participants’ sleep quality, as measured by the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index, will impact their final psychology exam grade

The correct answer is d. We hypothesize that participants’ sleep quality, as measured by the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index, will impact their final psychology exam grade. 

A non-directional hypothesis is a statement that predicts a difference or relationship between variables without specifying the direction of that difference or relationship. In this case, the hypothesis does not specify whether better sleep quality will lead to higher or lower exam grades; it only suggests that there is an impact.

Identify the scale of measurement for the following levels of experience: Professor, Senior Lecturer, Lecturer
Question 4Select one:

a.
Ratio

b.
Continuous

c.
Ordinal

d.
Interval

The correct answer is c. Ordinal.

Ordinal scale of measurement represents a type of categorical data where the values have a natural order or ranking. In this case, the levels of experience (Professor, Senior Lecturer, Lecturer) can be ranked or ordered based on their hierarchy or seniority. However, the intervals between the categories may not be consistent or evenly spaced, which is why it is considered an ordinal scale rather than an interval or ratio scale.

If the distribution of your variable was asymmetrical and the majority of your data points fall to the lower values of your x-axis, your data are:
Question 5Select one:

a.
Leptokurtic

b.
Gaussian

c.
Positively skewed

d.
Negatively skewed

The correct answer is c. Positively skewed.

Positively skewed data refers to a distribution in which the tail of the distribution extends towards higher values on the x-axis. In other words, the majority of the data points fall towards the lower values, and there are relatively few data points at the higher values. This results in a longer tail on the right side of the distribution. Therefore, if the distribution of the variable is asymmetrical and most data points are towards the lower values, it indicates a positively skewed distribution.

The aim of inferential statistics is: 



Question 6Select one:

a.
Taking a smaller sample to learn about a larger population


b.
Taking a larger sample to learn about a smaller population


c.
Taking a smaller population to learn about a larger sample


d.
Taking a larger population to learn about a smaller sample

The aim of inferential statistics is: 

a. Taking a smaller sample to learn about a larger population.

Inferential statistics involves using sample data to make inferences or draw conclusions about a larger population. By analyzing the sample data and applying statistical techniques, researchers can make predictions, test hypotheses, and generalize the findings to the larger population from which the sample was drawn. The goal is to make valid and reliable inferences about the population based on the information obtained from the smaller sample.

In your study, you find a significant difference between undergraduate and post graduate students’ sleep quality, but these findings are not actually true. This would be an example of a…
Question 7Select one:

a.
Correct result – no effect found

b.
Type 2 error

c.
Type 1 error

d.
Correct result – effect found

The correct answer is c. Type 1 error.

A Type 1 error occurs when the researcher rejects the null hypothesis when it is actually true. This means that the findings of a significant difference between undergraduate and postgraduate students' sleep quality would be considered significant, but in reality, there is no true difference. It represents a false positive result, indicating that the researcher incorrectly concludes that there is an effect or difference when there is not.

In this case, the researcher would have made a Type 1 error by incorrectly concluding that there is a significant difference in sleep quality between undergraduate and postgraduate students, when in fact, there is no true difference.

In an experiment, you test participants’ reaction time based on drinking alcoholic beer or non-alcoholic beer. From the given information, your independent variable is:
Question 8Select one:

a.
Reaction time

b.
Time of day

c.
Type of beer (alcoholic or non-alcoholic)

d.
Volume of liquid drank

The correct answer is c. Type of beer (alcoholic or non-alcoholic).

In the given information, the experiment tests participants' reaction time based on whether they consumed alcoholic beer or non-alcoholic beer. The independent variable, which is manipulated by the researcher, is the type of beer being consumed. It has two levels: alcoholic and non-alcoholic. The researcher is interested in examining how the different types of beer (independent variable) affect the participants' reaction time (dependent variable).

In the normal distribution:
Question 9Select one:

a.
The mean, median and mode are not the centre of the distribution

b.
The mean, median and mode are equal

c.
The mean will always be larger than both the median and mode

d.
The mean, median and mode are all 1SD apart

The correct answer is b. The mean, median, and mode are equal.

In a normal distribution, the mean, median, and mode are all equal and represent the center of the distribution. This is one of the defining characteristics of a symmetrical normal distribution. Regardless of the shape of the distribution or its spread, these three measures of central tendency will coincide with each other in a normal distribution, making them equal.

Which of the following is not a Questionable Research Practice?
Question 10Select one:

a.
Stopping data collection earlier than planned because the researcher found the result they had been looking for

b.
Determining sample size before beginning data collection

c.
Reporting an unexpected finding as having been predicted from the start

d.
Deciding whether to collect more data after looking to see whether the results were significant

The correct answer is b. Determining sample size before beginning data collection.

Determining the sample size before beginning data collection is not considered a questionable research practice. It is a standard practice in research design to calculate or estimate the appropriate sample size based on statistical power analysis or other considerations. This helps ensure that the study has a sufficient sample size to detect meaningful effects or relationships within the chosen population.

Questionable research practices refer to practices that can undermine the integrity or validity of research. These practices can include stopping data collection early due to finding desired results (a), reporting unexpected findings as having been predicted from the start (c), or deciding to collect more data based on the significance of preliminary results (d). These practices can introduce biases and affect the integrity of the research findings.

The presence of outliers is most likely to influence which of the following measures of dispersion:
Question 11Select one:

a.
Standard deviation

b.
Median

c.
Mean

d.
Interquartile range

The correct answer is a. Standard deviation.

Outliers have a significant impact on the measure of dispersion, particularly on the standard deviation. The standard deviation is a measure of how spread out the data is from the mean. Outliers, which are extreme values that deviate significantly from the majority of the data points, can greatly influence the calculation of the standard deviation. Since the standard deviation includes the squared deviations from the mean, the presence of outliers can increase the sum of squared deviations and result in a higher value for the standard deviation.

On the other hand, the median (b) and interquartile range (d) are resistant measures of dispersion that are less affected by outliers. The median is the middle value of a dataset when it is
